title: [ga-d5y] StoreMaintenanceLoop skeleton + lease mutex + lifecycle wiring
notes:
Child of ga-d5y. Blocked by ga-zol. Implements the scheduler goroutine
and its lease primitive, sibling to reconcileLoop. Design D1, D2, D10.

## Scope

Create internal/supervisor/maintenance.go with:

- type StoreMaintenanceLoop struct (cfg, store, cityPath, recorder,
  mu sync.Mutex, lastRunAt, history ring buffer size 16).
- func (m) Run(ctx) — goroutine loop with time.NewTimer and
  nextDelay(now) calculation; listen on ctx.Done.
- Jitter: ±10% on interval (interval * (0.9 + 0.2*rand.Float64())).
- func (m) runOnce(ctx) placeholder that respects the mutex and
  logs "would run maintenance" — real snapshot/gc/events land in
  beads 3-5.
- Lifecycle: wire Run into supervisor bootstrap so enabled=true
  launches it and disabled (or missing config) leaves it off.
- Load lastRunAt from event log (most recent
  gc.store.maintenance.done event for this city); if absent and
  interval >1.5× elapsed since startup, fire immediately.

## Files to touch

- internal/supervisor/maintenance.go (new).
- internal/supervisor/bootstrap.go (or equivalent) — launch goroutine.
- internal/supervisor/maintenance_test.go (new) — unit tests for
  nextDelay, jitter bounds, lease contention, ctx.Done shutdown.

## Acceptance

- Loop starts/stops cleanly on ctx.Done.
- Mutex-held → runOnce returns early without error.
- nextDelay test covers: lastRunAt zero-value, lastRunAt inside
  interval, lastRunAt outside interval.
- go test ./internal/supervisor/... green; no goroutine leaks
  (goleak.VerifyNone, or equivalent).

## Non-scope

- Actual snapshot / DOLT_GC / smoke test — beads 3-4.
- Event emission — bead 5.
- Alert mail — bead 6.
- API/CLI — bead 7.
- citystatus — bead 8.

## Pointers

- Prior art: internal/beads/caching_store_reconcile.go::reconcileLoop
- Design D1 (goroutine + scheduler choice), D2 (lease), D10 (cadence
  + jitter + lastRunAt persistence).
Implementation complete. Added:
- internal/supervisor/maintenance.go: StoreMaintenanceLoop + NewStoreMaintenanceLoop constructor; Run/nextDelay/applyJitter/runOnce/History/LastRunAt API; bounded 16-entry ring buffer; lease via sync.Mutex.TryLock
- SeedLastRunAt(events.Provider) helper: queries gc.store.maintenance.done, picks latest Ts; nil-safe; swallows errors (best-effort)
- Literal 'gc.store.maintenance.done' string with comment pointing to ga-8cq for constant swap
- Lifecycle wiring: controllerState.startMaintenanceLoop invoked from both runController (controller.go) and reconcileCities (cmd_supervisor.go); no-op when disabled

Tests (13 total): nextDelay {zero/inside/outside/default-weekly/jitter-min-mid-max}; runOnce {lease-contention/placeholder-updates}; Run {disabled-returns/ctx-cancel-exits-in-500ms}; History {bounded-at-16}; SeedLastRunAt {nil/empty/latest-of-many/ignores-unrelated/broken-provider}.

Goroutine leak check: used timeout pattern (prior art). goleak not vendored in this tree.

go test ./internal/supervisor/... green; go vet clean; go build ./... clean. Pre-existing TestControllerStateLegacyFileProviderSharesRigStoreHandle failure confirmed unrelated (fails on HEAD without my changes).

Out-of-scope items remain for follow-on beads: snapshot/DOLT_GC/smoke (ga-74d, ga-zoj), event registration+emission (ga-8cq), alert mail (ga-p5n), CLI/API (ga-zn8), citystatus (ga-e3s), runbook (ga-sec).

title: [ga-d5y] Add [maintenance.dolt] config section + parser + defaults
notes:
Child of ga-d5y. Adds config plumbing that the rest of the
maintenance decomposition consumes. Design: append-note on ga-d5y
(D1, D7, D10). Plan: docs/plans/ga-d5y-dolt-maintenance-runbook.md.

## Scope

Add a new TOML section and its parsed struct:

```toml
[maintenance.dolt]
enabled = false         # opt-in for v1
interval = "168h"       # weekly; ADR-suggested default
alert_to = ""           # empty → no mail; set e.g. "gascity/mayor"
gc_timeout = "10m"      # ceiling for CALL DOLT_GC()
```

## Files to touch

- internal/config/ (parser for the new section). Follow an existing
  section's pattern — unit tests in <pkg>_test.go.
- internal/config/defaults or wherever default values are centralized.
- internal/config/types.go (or equivalent) — DoltMaintenance struct.

## Acceptance

- Parsing accepts the section; omission = disabled (no error).
- Duration fields parse with time.ParseDuration; invalid durations
  surface a typed error at load time, not runtime.
- go test ./internal/config/... green.
- No consumer of the new struct exists yet (scheduler lands in the
  next bead).

## User-confirmation defaults

- enabled = false (designer's choice; confirmed with user via mail
  gm-wisp-y5i unless overridden).
- interval = "168h" (weekly; user may override to 336h).
- alert_to = "" (user may set default to "gascity/mayor" in
  gascity's shipped city.toml template).

## Pointers

- Design D1 (scheduler), D7 (alert_to), D10 (cadence).
- Plan: docs/plans/ga-d5y-dolt-maintenance-runbook.md
## Builder handoff (commit 67cdfb34)

Shipped:

- `MaintenanceConfig` + `DoltMaintenance` structs in
  `internal/config/config.go`; `City.Maintenance` field added after
  `Convergence`.
- `IntervalOrDefault()` → 168h fallback.
- `GCTimeoutOrDefault()` → 10m fallback.
- Fragment merge: last-writer-wins if fragment defines
  `[maintenance]` (mirrors the `convergence` pattern).
- `ValidateDurations` now checks `[maintenance.dolt]` `interval`
  and `gc_timeout` — invalid durations produce a warning at load
  time via the same warning channel every other section uses.
- Tests (all green, `go test ./internal/config/... -count=1`):
  - `TestParseMaintenanceDoltFullSection` — round-trips the full
    four-field section.
  - `TestParseMaintenanceDoltOmittedSection` — omission yields
    zero-valued struct, no error.
  - `TestParseMaintenanceDoltPartialSection` — partial section
    leaves other fields empty (defaults are applied at use time,
    not parse time, matching existing `ChatSessions` /
    `Convergence` style).
  - `TestDoltMaintenanceIntervalOrDefault` — 4 subtests covering
    empty / valid / invalid.
  - `TestDoltMaintenanceGCTimeoutOrDefault` — same matrix.
  - `TestValidateDurationsBadMaintenanceDoltFields` — bad
    duration produces two warnings (interval + gc_timeout) each
    mentioning `[maintenance.dolt]`, the field name, and the bad
    value.
  - `TestValidateDurationsMaintenanceDoltValidOK` — negative case.

## Deviation from spec

The bead says "invalid durations surface a typed error at load
time, not runtime." The existing `ValidateDurations` convention
used by every other duration field surfaces these as *warnings*
from `ValidateDurations(cfg, source) []string`, not as typed
errors that fail load. I followed the existing convention rather
than inventing a parallel typed-error path. If the reviewer /
ga-8km author wants stricter behavior, promoting these warnings
to load errors is a separate change with repo-wide implications.

## For ga-8km consumer

- Read `cfg.Maintenance.Dolt.Enabled` to gate loop start.
- Use `cfg.Maintenance.Dolt.IntervalOrDefault()` for the timer
  base (apply ±10% jitter per the designer's D10).
- Use `cfg.Maintenance.Dolt.GCTimeoutOrDefault()` as the
  `context.WithTimeout` ceiling around `CALL DOLT_GC()`.
- `cfg.Maintenance.Dolt.AlertTo` empty → skip mail, per ga-p5n.

No API / OpenAPI surface was touched (config is not reflected in
the wire schema). No events registered (ga-8cq owns those).
close_reason: Config plumbing shipped in commit 67cdfb34; handoff note attached. Ready for review.

title: Review: BEADS_ACTOR/GC_AGENT alignment fix (ga-6fo)
description:
Review the fix for ga-6fo: align BEADS_ACTOR with GC_AGENT in named-session env overrides.

## What landed

Commit 668eeaa1 on branch gc-builder-1-01561d4fb9ea.

Two-line code change (each site adds a BEADS_ACTOR identity assignment next to the existing GC_AGENT/GC_ALIAS/GC_TEMPLATE overrides):
- cmd/gc/build_desired_state.go:386-393 — named-session loop in buildDesiredState
- cmd/gc/session_reconciler.go:1095-1102 — resolveTemplateForLiveNamedSession

Two new phase0 spec tests in cmd/gc/session_model_phase0_runtime_env_spec_test.go:
- TestPhase0RuntimeEnv_EphemeralPoolAgentBEADSActorMatchesSessionName — regression guard for pool/ephemeral invariant (already satisfied by template_resolve.go)
- TestPhase0RuntimeEnv_NamedSingletonBEADSActorMatchesIdentity — locks the fixed invariant; fails pre-fix with BEADS_ACTOR="gascity--mayor" want "gascity/mayor"

Docs: created docs/reference/session-env.md with the BEADS_ACTOR convention table and linked from docs/reference/index.md. Note: the spec called for adding to docs/reference/cli.md, but cli.md is auto-generated from the Cobra command tree ("Auto-generated — do not edit"), so the hand-maintained content lives in session-env.md instead.

## Verification

- go test on the two new tests: PASS before and after fix for the ephemeral one; FAIL→PASS for the named one (confirmed TDD).
- Full go test ./cmd/gc/... ./internal/session/... has 5 failures that reproduce on HEAD without this patch — pre-existing branch state, not regressions from this change.
- go vet ./cmd/gc/ ./internal/session/: clean
- go build ./cmd/gc/: clean

## Outstanding for manual verification

The done-when checklist in ga-6fo includes a manual smoke test after gc rebuild: for a running named session, BEADS_ACTOR == GC_AGENT == GC_TEMPLATE should hold when inspected via /proc/$PID/environ. This requires restarting a named session after the binary rebuild.

## Out-of-scope items (explicitly not touched per spec)

- Pool/ephemeral BEADS_ACTOR format unchanged
- template_resolve.go:223 unchanged
- RuntimeEnvWithSessionContext unchanged
- No investigation into the "sling didn't auto-spawn" note in ga-dre
notes:
REVIEW VERDICT: PASS (first-pass reviewer — handing to gemini-reviewer)

Scope verified: commit 668eeaa1 on gc-builder-1-01561d4fb9ea.

Style / Build
- `go vet ./cmd/gc/ ./internal/session/`: clean.
- `go build ./cmd/gc/`: clean.
- Imports in the new test additions (beads, runtime) resolve cleanly
  (beads.NewMemStore at internal/beads/memstore.go:23; runtime.NewFake
  at internal/runtime/fake.go:64; intPtr already exists at
  cmd/gc/pool_desired_state_test.go:10).
- Added comment lines cite the source investigation (ga-dre) — keeps
  future readers pointed at the why.

Security / OWASP
- No new trust boundary. `identity` flows from
  `cfg.NamedSessions[i].QualifiedName()` (build_desired_state.go:303)
  and is already used for GC_ALIAS/GC_AGENT/GC_TEMPLATE in the same
  override block. BEADS_ACTOR joining that set is at the same trust
  level — not a shell interpolation, not a new sink.
- No logging, authZ, or permissions change. No deserialization. No
  external inputs newly surfaced.

Spec compliance (ga-6fo "Done-when" checklist)
- [x] build_desired_state.go named-session loop sets
      `tp.Env["BEADS_ACTOR"] = identity` (line 393).
- [x] session_reconciler.go `resolvePreservedConfiguredNamedSessionTemplate`
      sets the same (line 1102).
- [x] New phase0 test asserts BEADS_ACTOR == GC_AGENT == GC_TEMPLATE for
      a named singleton (TestPhase0RuntimeEnv_NamedSingletonBEADSActorMatchesIdentity).
- [x] New phase0 test asserts BEADS_ACTOR == GC_SESSION_NAME == GC_AGENT
      for pool/ephemeral (TestPhase0RuntimeEnv_EphemeralPoolAgentBEADSActorMatchesSessionName).
- [x] Both new tests PASS (`go test -run 'TestPhase0RuntimeEnv_(Ephemeral…|NamedSingleton…)'`).
- [~] Docs: spec step 4 named `docs/reference/cli.md`, but cli.md
      carries an "Auto-generated — do not edit" banner. Builder
      correctly placed hand-maintained content in a new
      `docs/reference/session-env.md` and linked it under
      "Hand-Maintained Reference Docs" in `docs/reference/index.md`.
      Reasonable deviation; content matches the spec table verbatim.
- [ ] Manual smoke (rebuild + restart named session + inspect
      /proc/$PID/environ) — not verified here; belongs to release-gate.

Test-suite regressions
- Builder claimed "5 pre-existing failures" in
  `go test ./cmd/gc/... ./internal/session/...`; I observed more
  failing top-level tests (~17), BUT none touch the files changed by
  this commit (`git diff HEAD~1 HEAD -- <failing-test-files>` is
  empty). The failures are environmental / unrelated and predate this
  patch. Non-regression claim stands; the count was understated but
  not material to this review.

Out-of-scope items (per spec, correctly untouched):
- Pool/ephemeral BEADS_ACTOR format unchanged.
- template_resolve.go:223 unchanged.
- RuntimeEnvWithSessionContext unchanged.
- "sling didn't auto-spawn" note from ga-dre intentionally deferred.

Findings list:
- INFO / docs-deviation / low confidence / docs/reference/session-env.md:1
  Hand-maintained content placed in session-env.md rather than cli.md
  because cli.md is auto-generated. Reasonable; no action.
- INFO / test-claim-understated / low confidence / N/A
  Commit message/handoff claimed 5 pre-existing failures; actual
  count ~17. Not a regression — none touch changed files — but worth
  noting so future reviewers don't assume the baseline is cleaner
  than it is.

No blockers. Advancing to second-pass gemini-reviewer before release-gate.

title: [ga-h6w] Route cmd_beads.go (list, show) through supervisor API
notes:
## Implementation plan (builder-4, session 1776949308)

Scoping disposition: adopting builder-1's interpretation. Per plan doc
docs/plans/ga-h6w-read-path-api-routing.md row #9: 'migrate-cmd-beads |
cmd_beads.go (bead list, show)'. The bead_format.go helpers are
pre-staged for these commands. The genclient has
GetV0CityByCityNameBeads and GetV0CityByCityNameBeadById. Humandlers
humaHandleBeadList and humaHandleBeadGet exist. Adding new 'gc beads
list' and 'gc beads show <id>' subcommands with API routing + bd-store
fallback aligns with sibling closed beads (ga-06g convoy, ga-6q1 order,
ga-2o9 rig).

Steps (TDD, modeled after cmd_rig.go + cmd_convoy.go):
1. Add cacheLiveOr503 gate + CacheAgeS in humaHandleBeadList and
   humaHandleBeadGet.
2. Create decode_beads.go with beadsFromGenList translator + tests
   (reuse beadFromGen from decode_convoys.go).
3. Add ListBeads / GetBead wrappers to internal/api/client.go with
   CachedRead envelope.
4. Add newBeadsListCmd / newBeadsShowCmd to cmd_beads.go (with
   routeBeadsList / routeBeadsShow dispatchers, apiClient + logRoute +
   ShouldFallbackForRead + doBeads{List,Show} bd-store fallback).
5. Six-row matrix tests for both commands in cmd_beads_test.go.
6. Run scripts/check-routed-test-rows.sh, go test ./..., go vet.

## Review verdict: request-changes

Reviewer: gascity/reviewer
Commit: d0b5dd41 (ga-gti)
Date: 2026-04-23

### Blocking

**B1. `--all` flag has inverted logic — `gc beads list --all` exits 1.**
- File: `cmd/gc/cmd_beads.go`, `collectBeadsAcrossStores` (~L293)
- Code: `if !filters.all { q.AllowScan = true }`
- Effect: Without `--all` → AllowScan=true, query accepted. With `--all`
  and no other filters → AllowScan=false, HasFilter()=false, bdstore
  rejects with `bd list: bead query requires scan`. Command exits 1.
- Reproduced: `doBeadsListFallback(cityPath, "text", beadFilters{all: true}, ...)`
  returned code=1, stderr=`"gc beads list: bd list: bead query requires scan"`.
- `--all` is advertised in `newBeadsListCmd.Long`: "Supports --label,
  --status, --all, and --format flags." The new six-row test matrix
  doesn't exercise it, so CI didn't catch this.
- Fix options:
  1. Invert: `if filters.all { q.AllowScan = true }` (making --all the
     explicit scan opt-in) and require filters otherwise.
  2. If `--all` should mean "include closed", set
     `q.IncludeClosed = filters.all` and keep AllowScan unconditional.
  3. Drop `--all` from the help text if no semantics are defined.
  Please also add a test row that exercises the chosen semantics.

### Non-blocking concerns

**C1. API path drops `filters.all` entirely.** `api.ListBeadsOpts` has
only `{Status, Type, Label, Assignee, Rig, Limit}`. Once B1 is fixed,
API vs fallback parity for `--all` needs attention — otherwise the two
paths diverge silently.

**C2. Double-filtering on API path.** `renderBeadsListFromAPI` calls
`filterBeads(cr.Body, filters)` after the server already filtered via
`ListBeadsOpts`. Redundant; any divergence between server-side and
client-side filter logic is masked.

**C3. Silent 50-bead truncation on API path.** Server defaults to
`Limit: 50` (`internal/api/huma_handlers_beads.go:23`). CLI has no
`--limit` flag, so `gc beads list` via API silently returns at most 50
beads with no indication of truncation; fallback returns all. Not
introduced by this commit but surfaced by routing to it.

**C4. `--format=toon` is in the help example but falls through to
text.** Acknowledged in commit message as pre-existing behavior.
Consider dropping from the example or adding a TOON writer in a
follow-up.

### What's good

- Six-row matrix pattern matches closed siblings (ga-06g, ga-6q1, ga-2o9).
- `cacheLiveOr503` gate + `X-GC-Cache-Age-S` header wiring is correct.
- Decoder tests cover nil body, nil items, empty slice, sparse fields,
  and nil pointer.
- `writeBead{s,}JSONWithCache` envelope correctly gated to API path only.
- `go vet ./...` clean.
- `go test ./cmd/gc/ -run 'TestRouteBeads|TestDoBeads'` passes.
- `go test ./internal/api/ -run 'TestOpenAPISpecInSync|TestEveryKnownEventTypeHasRegisteredPayload|TestBead'` passes.
- `scripts/check-routed-test-rows.sh` reports zero missing rows.

### Security

OWASP Top 10 sweep — no new findings. Bead ID flows to `GetBead` via
the genclient, which URL-encodes path params. No direct SQL/shell
injection surface introduced. No new auth/authz changes. No sensitive
data exposure in logs (route logging is gated on `GC_DEBUG`).

comments.author: builder-gm-fj7yydu
comments.text: Builder note (2026-04-23): scoping ambiguity surfaced. Bead title and Files-to-touch point at cmd/gc/cmd_beads.go, but that file exposes only 'gc beads city' and 'gc beads health' subcommands — no 'list'/'show'. The 'bead list'/'bead show' operations currently live in cmd/gc/cmd_bd.go, which is a DisableFlagParsing passthrough that execs the bd binary. Two possible interpretations: (a) add new first-class 'gc beads list' and 'gc beads show' subcommands in cmd_beads.go that API-route with fallback to 'gc bd list/show'; or (b) intercept 'list'/'show' inside the existing gc-bd passthrough. Path (b) is tricky because bd supports many filter flags (--live, --metadata-field, --limit, --sort, etc.) that do not 1:1 map to the GetV0CityByCityNameBeadsParams surface, so routing would need conservative 'simple-args-only' gating with fallback on any unknown flag. Path (a) avoids that but duplicates UX with gc-bd. Releasing this bead for PM/designer clarification while I continue with the cobra-command migrations (ga-2fr, ga-69s, ga-6s5, etc.) which have unambiguous scope.

comments.author: builder-gm-z5z9cfh
comments.text: Builder-2 note (2026-04-23): Scoping ambiguity from prior builder still unresolved. cmd/gc/cmd_beads.go exposes only 'gc beads city' and 'gc beads health' — no list/show. The list/show operations live in cmd/gc/cmd_bd.go as a DisableFlagParsing passthrough to the bd binary. Need PM/designer disposition on path-a (new gc beads list/show subcommands) vs path-b (intercept in gc-bd passthrough) before a builder can proceed. Releasing back to PM. Claiming ga-2fr instead.

comments.author: gascity--builder
comments.text: Rework plan (builder, session 2026-04-23): Applying Option 2 — --all means IncludeClosed (parity with 'bd list --all'). Addresses both B1 (inverted logic) and C1 (API parity). Changes: (1) collectBeadsAcrossStores: unconditional AllowScan, IncludeClosed=filters.all; (2) BeadListInput: add 'All bool query:all'; (3) humaHandleBeadList: set query.IncludeClosed=input.All; (4) ListBeadsOpts: add All bool; (5) client.ListBeads: plumb to genclient params.All; (6) routeBeadsList: pass All: filters.all; (7) regen openapi.json + genclient. Tests: add --all rows to six-row matrix verifying API query param + fallback IncludeClosed behavior. C2/C3/C4 deferred to follow-up bead.

title: [ga-h6w] Route cmd_order.go (history) through supervisor API
notes:
Per-file migration bead for ga-h6w (ADR 0001). Blocked by enabler
ga-71l (scaffolding). Migrate `cmd_order.go` read commands to route through
the supervisor API with transparent fallback.

## Commands in scope

- order history

## Files to touch

- cmd/gc/cmd_order.go — route through apiClient + ShouldFallback; call logRoute
  exactly once per exit path.
- cmd/gc/cmd_order_test.go — add six mandatory table-driven rows.
- internal/api/client.go — add typed read wrapper(s) for the endpoints
  in scope (pattern from enabler D1).
- internal/api/decode_orders.go (new or extend) — translator(s) for
  genclient response → CLI-facing type. Cover valid / empty list /
  partial-missing-field in tests.
- internal/api/types_read.go — add stable CLI types mirrored 1:1 with
  existing human-facing formatter output.
- internal/api/handler_orders.go — ensure IsLive() gate (from enabler)
  applies; no new 503 logic per command.

## Six-row test matrix (mandatory)

Each routed command has these six table-driven rows in its *_test.go:

| Row | serverHandler | useEscapeHatch | wantExitCode | wantRouteLog |
|-----|---------------|----------------|--------------|--------------|
| api-happy-path     | ok         | false | 0 | route=api |
| api-cache-not-live | 503 typed  | false | 0 | route=fallback reason=cache-not-live |
| api-500-fallback   | 5xx        | false | 0 | route=fallback reason=conn-refused |
| api-404-error      | 404        | false | 1 | (stderr: not found) |
| controller-down    | nil server | false | 0 | route=fallback reason=controller-down |
| escape-hatch       | ok         | true  | 0 | route=fallback reason=escape-hatch |

Fallback rows also assert the fallback path produces correct output
(stub BdStore or fixture dir).

## Acceptance

- go test ./cmd/gc/... ./internal/api/... green.
- go vet ./... clean.
- --json output on API path contains _cache_age_s; fallback omits it.
- Human output > 30 s stale (API path) appends cache-age banner.
- Zero new genclient imports in cmd/gc/.
- scripts/check-routed-test-rows.sh (from enabler) reports zero
  missing rows for this file.

## Pointers

- Design: append-note on ga-h6w (D1, D2, D3, D7 most relevant here).
- Plan: docs/plans/ga-h6w-read-path-api-routing.md
- Enabler contract reference: ga-71l notes.

comments.author: builder-gm-fj7yydu
comments.text:
Implementation complete (2026-04-23).

Files touched:
- internal/api/huma_handlers_orders.go: added cacheLiveOr503 gate + CacheAgeS header on OrderHistoryListOutput.
- internal/api/decode_orders.go (new): OrderHistoryView type + genclient translator.
- internal/api/decode_orders_test.go (new): valid/empty/partial-missing-field tests.
- internal/api/client.go: Client.GetOrderHistory(scopedName, limit, before) returning CachedRead[[]OrderHistoryView].
- cmd/gc/cmd_order.go: routeOrderHistory dispatcher + renderOrderHistoryFromAPI + orderScopedName helper + orderHistoryAPIClient indirection var.
- cmd/gc/cmd_order_test.go: six-row matrix + MultiOrderFallback + StaleBannerOver30s + orderScopedName unit test.
- internal/api/openapi.json, docs/schema/openapi.{json,txt}: regenerated by go generate ./internal/api/... (new X-GC-Cache-Age-S header on /orders/history).

Scope decisions:

1. Multi-order case ('gc order history' with no name): falls back to the local iterator, logged as 'route=fallback reason=multi-order'. The /orders/history endpoint is scoped_name-required (server returns 400 when empty), so aggregating across orders would require N API calls per invocation. Deferred for now; the deliberate fallback is audit-logged so operators see the branch.

2. --json flag: gc order history has no --json flag today; the bead acceptance item '--json output on API path contains _cache_age_s' is non-applicable for this command. The other half of the acceptance ('> 30 s stale banner on human output') is satisfied.

3. scoped_name composition: when a matching order is loaded locally (via findOrder), its ScopedName() is authoritative (correctly distinguishes city-level 'foo' vs rig-level 'foo:rig:<rig>'). When no match, composition falls back to inputs ('name' alone for empty rig, 'name:rig:<rig>' otherwise). This preserves existing CLI semantics (first-match by name), just with an API round-trip instead of a local bead scan.

## Acceptance checks

- go test ./internal/api/... green.
- go test ./cmd/gc/ -run 'TestRouteOrderHistory|TestOrderScopedName|TestOrderList' green (13 new tests).
- go vet ./... clean.
- scripts/check-routed-test-rows.sh exits 0.
- TestOpenAPISpecInSync green (regenerated spec matches live Huma).
- Zero new genclient imports in cmd/gc/ (verified by grep).

## Pre-existing test noise (unrelated)

- TestCmdOrderHistoryUsesProviderAwareCityStore / TestCmdOrderRunExec* / TestCmdOrderRunFormulaUsesProviderAwareCityStore: 'rig "gascity" is not registered in any city' — reproduces at merge base 99261cd2 with same env. Not caused by this change.
- Disk-quota flakes on /tmp in the full cmd/gc test run: environmental, pre-existing.

## Other beads encountered (deferred, comments on each)

- ga-gti (beads list/show): cmd_beads.go has no list/show; the operations live in cmd_bd.go as a bd-binary passthrough. Needs PM/designer call on (a) add first-class 'gc beads list'/'show' or (b) intercept inside the gc-bd passthrough.
- ga-2fr (wait list/inspect): /v0/city/{name}/waits endpoint does not exist in openapi.json — needs scaffolding before the CLI migration can land.
- ga-69s (citystatus + status): /v0/status returns aggregate counts only; 'gc status' emits per-agent detail — shape mismatch. Also 'gc citystatus' is not a registered command.

Ready for reviewer handoff.

comments.author: builder-gm-fj7yydu
comments.text: Builder note (2026-04-23): shape mismatch between CLI output and API response. 'gc status' currently emits per-agent detail (name, running state, suspension, pool) and per-rig detail via StatusJSON. The /v0/status endpoint returns aggregate counts only (StatusAgentCounts{Total,Running,Suspended,Quarantined}, StatusRigCounts{Total,Suspended}) — no per-agent or per-rig list. Routing the CLI through the API would drop per-agent/per-rig detail on the API path, producing different output vs fallback. Also: there's no 'gc citystatus' command in main.go (only 'gc status') despite the bead listing both. Two resolutions needed: (a) extend /v0/status to include agent/rig details, or (b) accept two output shapes depending on controller liveness. Releasing for PM/architect decision. Picking up ga-6q1 (order history) which should have a cleaner 1:1 API-CLI mapping.

comments.author: builder-gm-z5z9cfh
comments.text: Builder-2 note (2026-04-23): Scoping gap. The existing /v0/status handler (internal/api/handler_status.go) returns aggregate counts only (StatusAgentCounts, StatusRigCounts, StatusWorkCounts, StatusMailCounts, StoreHealth), while cmd_citystatus.go renders per-agent records (QualifiedName, Pool, Scope, Running, Suspended), per-rig records (Name, Path, Suspended), named sessions, and ControllerJSON state — none of which live on /v0/status. Also, the bead claims cmd_status.go (which defines 'gc rig status <name>') shares the /status handler, but 'gc rig status' observes per-agent runtime state for one rig, which /v0/status does not expose. Per the plan, 'new read-endpoint Huma handlers beyond the 16 existing genclient methods' is out of scope, so the required data enrichment is blocked. Needs PM/designer disposition: (a) extend /v0/status to include per-record detail, (b) accept behavior change where API path shows aggregate-only, (c) split cmd_status.go out as a separate bead, or (d) drop from ga-h6w. Releasing back to PM.

comments.author: builder-gm-qf7e40c
comments.text:
Builder plan (2026-04-23):
1. Expand StatusBody with additive detail fields (ControllerDetail, AgentDetails, RigDetails, NamedSessionDetails, SessionCountsDetail). Non-breaking.
2. humaHandleStatus: add cacheLiveOr503 gate + populate IndexOutput.CacheAgeS from cacheAgeSeconds(store). Update buildStatusBody to populate detail fields in addition to counts.
3. Regenerate openapi.json + genclient.
4. types_read.go: add StatusView, StatusControllerView, StatusAgentView, StatusRigView, StatusNamedSessionView, StatusSessionCountsView.
5. decode_status.go: translator genclient.StatusBody -> StatusView.
6. Client.GetStatus() returning CachedRead[StatusView].
7. cmd_citystatus.go: routeCityStatus + routeCityStatusJSON + renderCityStatusFromAPI + renderCityStatusJSONFromAPI. Six-row matrix test.
8. cmd_status.go: routeRigStatus + rigStatusAPIClient + renderRigStatusFromAPI. Filter AgentDetails by rig server-side (or client-side). Six-row matrix test.
9. Human output appends stale banner when cache age > 30 s on API path; JSON adds _cache_age_s.
10. Run go test ./cmd/gc/... ./internal/api/... + go vet + scripts/check-routed-test-rows.sh.

comments.author: builder-gm-fj7yydu
comments.text: Builder note (2026-04-23): the API does not expose a /v0/city/{name}/waits endpoint — openapi.json lists zero wait-specific paths, and there is no handler_waits.go in internal/api. The bead's Files-to-touch assumes an existing handler ('ensure IsLive gate applies'), but the endpoint itself is missing. This migration would require scaffolding both a new Huma handler + CLI routing. Releasing so the PM/architect can decide whether to add the endpoint as a separate enabler or fold it into this bead. Picking up ga-69s (citystatus + status) meanwhile — /v0/city/{name}/status already exists.

comments.author: builder-gm-z5z9cfh
comments.text: Builder-2 note (2026-04-23): ga-2fr also has a scope gap. The bead says to 'ensure IsLive() gate (from enabler) applies' on internal/api/handler_waits.go, but no wait HTTP endpoints exist in the API at all — there is no handler_waits.go, huma_handlers_wait*.go, or genclient GetV0CityByCityName*Wait* method. cmdWaitList and cmdWaitInspect currently query the local bead store directly. Routing these through the supervisor API first requires building out the server endpoints (handler, huma registration, OpenAPI regen, genclient regen), which is a larger scope than the ga-h6w per-file routing pattern assumes. Needs designer/PM disposition: either spec the new wait API surface as part of this bead or split out an enabler. Releasing back to PM. Claiming ga-69s (status, handlers already exist) instead.

comments.author: builder-gm-fq2s3rl
comments.text: Builder-3 note (2026-04-23): Confirming the scope gap flagged by builder-1 and builder-2. Verified: openapi.json has zero /v0/.../waits paths (only the 'wait' query param on unrelated endpoints), internal/api has no handler_waits.go and no huma_handlers_wait*.go, genclient has no GetV0CityByCityName*Wait* methods. cmdWaitList/cmdWaitInspect currently hit the local bead store directly. Routing these requires net-new server scaffolding (handler + huma + OpenAPI regen + genclient regen) — bigger than the per-file pattern in ga-h6w. Re-routing to architect to decide: (a) spec this as a separate enabler bead (parallel to ga-71l) and keep ga-2fr paused, or (b) expand ga-2fr's scope to include the server work, or (c) close ga-2fr if wait commands stay on the local bead store. No other routed builder work in the pool.

title: [ga-h6w] Enabler: api.Client read wrappers, route logger, cache-not-live contract
notes:
Scaffolding that unblocks all per-file read-path migrations under
parent bead ga-h6w. Implements ADR 0001 mechanics described in the
designer's append-note on ga-h6w. Plan doc:
docs/plans/ga-h6w-read-path-api-routing.md.

## Files to touch (new/modified)

- internal/api/client.go — add read-wrapper method pattern (see D1
  code sketch); ShouldFallback gains *cacheNotLiveError clause.
- internal/api/types_read.go (new) — CLI-facing stable types mirrored
  1:1 with existing BdStore formatter shapes.
- internal/api/decode_<domain>.go (new; scaffolding for
  sessions/mail/rig/convoy/wait/status/order/beads) — translators from
  generated response types to CLI types. Per-file beads add the
  remaining translators.
- internal/api/handler_*.go — add IsLive() gate that returns
  huma.Error503ServiceUnavailable with body {"type":
  "cache-not-live", ...} when the cache is priming.
- cmd/gc/apiroute.go — GC_NO_API env-gate at top of
  apiClient(cityPath); logRoute(cmd, route, reason, extra...)
  helper emitting key=value stderr lines.
- scripts/check-routed-test-rows.sh (new) — grep-based lint
  asserting every cmd_*_test.go with a routed command has the six
  mandatory table rows; wire into Makefile/CI.

## Contracts to lock

1. Escape hatch. GC_NO_API truthy (1/true/yes, case-insensitive) →
   apiClient returns nil + logRoute(cmd, fallback, escape-hatch).
   Empty/unset → normal path. Unknown value → warn + treat as unset
   (fail-open).
2. Envelope. API-path JSON adds top-level _cache_age_s float;
   fallback-path JSON omits the field. Human output appends
   "(cache age: Ns — reconciler may be lagging)" only when age > 30 s
   AND the API path was taken.
3. 503 body. {"status": 503, "title": "Cache not yet live",
   "detail": "Supervisor cache is priming or reconciling; retry via
   fallback.", "type": "cache-not-live"}. Verify against vendored
   Huma v2 typed-error API; if the exact "type" key is not
   expressible, propose an equivalent and note it on the bead before
   landing.
4. Reason codes (closed set). controller-down, conn-refused,
   cache-not-live, read-only, client-init, escape-hatch,
   non-loopback-bind. Non-fallbackable errors print stderr and exit
   non-zero; no fallback log line.
5. Translator placement. No genclient types cross the
   internal/api/ boundary into cmd/gc/. Enforce by having the
   per-domain decode files be the only consumers of
   internal/api/genclient/*.

## Mandatory tests

- Unit tests for each decode scaffold: valid, empty list,
  partial/missing field.
- Unit test for ShouldFallback covering *cacheNotLiveError.
- Unit test for GC_NO_API env-gate (truthy, empty, unknown).
- scripts/check-routed-test-rows.sh CI lint itself.
- Integration test demonstrating 503 cache-not-live → fallback path
  against httptest.Server pointed at the new handler.

## Acceptance

- go test ./internal/api/... ./cmd/gc/... green.
- go vet ./... clean.
- No new hand-written JSON on the wire path.
- TestOpenAPISpecInSync passes (handler 503 additions regenerate
  OpenAPI spec).
- TestEveryKnownEventTypeHasRegisteredPayload unaffected (no new
  events in this bead).
- CI lint script reports zero missing rows.

## Pointers

- Design: append-note on ga-h6w.
- Plan: /home/jaword/projects/gascity/docs/plans/ga-h6w-read-path-api-routing.md
- ADR: docs/adr/0001-gc-read-path-routes-through-supervisor.md
- Architecture: docs/architecture/gc-read-path.md (sections AF-1, AF-2)
- Rule: docs/rules/gc-read-path-routes-through-api.md
- Prior art: cmd/gc/cmd_suspend.go:65, cmd/gc/cmd_agent.go:552,
  cmd/gc/apiroute.go:20, cmd/gc/cmd_events_test.go

## User-confirmation pending

D5 _cache_age_s envelope addition is potentially breaking if any
closed-schema gc ... --json consumer exists. Builder: if
gm-wisp-y5i (PM → mayor) has not yet been answered by the time you
claim this, nudge the PM before landing. Otherwise ship as specified.

comments.author: builder-gm-i2dxiuo
comments.text:
Implementation plan (builder, 2026-04-22):

Decomposing the enabler into logical chunks, landed in one branch but separate commits for reviewability:

1. client.go: cacheNotLiveError type + ShouldFallback clause + unit test.
2. cmd/gc/apiroute.go: GC_NO_API env gate (truthy=fallback, unknown=warn+unset) + test.
3. cmd/gc/route_log.go (new): logRoute(cmd, route, reason, extra...) helper writing key=value to stderr (gated on GC_DEBUG); test.
4. CachingStore cache-age helper: expose LastReconcileAt via existing Stats(); add internal/api helper cacheAgeSeconds() on Server.
5. Response header X-GC-Cache-Age-S on ListOutput/IndexOutput. Server populates from (4). CLI client reads and surfaces as _cache_age_s in --json output + > 30s banner on human output (CLI-side construction, wire stays clean).
6. Handler IsLive gate: internal/api helper cacheLiveOr503(store) returning huma.Error503ServiceUnavailable with typed 'cache-not-live' body. Wire into the 8 read handlers that per-file beads will migrate (sessions list/get, mail list/count/get, rigs list, convoys list/get/check, beads list/get, orders history, status). Unit tests.
7. api.Client: classify 503 with 'cache-not-live' body into *cacheNotLiveError (already in ShouldFallback via 1). Unit test.
8. internal/api/types_read.go: package declaration + any shared envelope helpers. Domain-specific types land in per-file beads.
9. internal/api/decode_{sessions,mail,rig,convoys,waits,status,orders,beads}.go: scaffolding files w/ package declaration + empty translator stub for 'valid/empty/partial-missing' tests. Per-file beads fill in the translators.
10. scripts/check-routed-test-rows.sh: grep lint asserting every cmd_*_test.go with a routed command has the six named rows. Wire into Makefile 'lint' target.
11. Integration test: httptest.Server + api.Client exercising happy path, 503 cache-not-live → ShouldFallback==true, 5xx → ShouldFallback==true, 404 → non-fallback.

Resolved builder-resolvable item (D8): huma v2 uses huma.NewError to include typed fields; I'll use huma.Error503ServiceUnavailable with a 'cache-not-live:' prefix on the detail string (verified parse-ability). If a richer typed body is needed downstream, this slot is extensible.

Decision log:
- _cache_age_s: header on the wire, CLI-side envelope construction. Keeps Huma types clean; no need to modify every response Body type.
- IsLive gate: shared helper applied at handler entry, not middleware. Per-handler call keeps the surface explicit and the 503 typed.
- Scaffolding files (types_read.go + decode_*.go): created with package stubs only; per-file beads own their translators.

Scope guard: this enabler does NOT route any CLI command. Per-file beads do. Only exception: the integration test may wire one command end-to-end to prove the contract works.
